# DBMLX Language

`.dbmlx` is a superset of DBML (Database Markup Language). All valid DBML is valid `.dbmlx`. Extensions: `!include` for multi-file schemas, `DiagramView` for named filtered views, and migration diff annotations (`[add]`, `[drop]`, `[modify:]`).

Files use the `.dbmlx` extension. The VSCode extension (DBMLX) renders them as interactive diagrams.

Full reference: `docs/language-reference.md`

---

## Core syntax (inherited from DBML)

```dbmlx
Table schema.table_name {
  id          int           [pk, increment]
  name        varchar(255)  [not null]
  email       text          [unique]
  amount      decimal(10,2) [default: 0]
  note        text          [null, note: 'optional']
  created_at  timestamp     [default: `now()`]

  indexes {
    (email)       [unique, name: "uq_email"]
    (name, email) [name: "idx_name_email"]
  }

  Note: 'Table-level description'
}

// Refs (foreign keys)
Ref: orders.user_id > users.id       // many-to-one
Ref: users.id < orders.user_id       // one-to-many
Ref: users.id - profiles.user_id     // one-to-one
Ref: posts.id <> tags.id             // many-to-many
Ref: orders.(user_id, org_id) > users.(id, org_id)  // composite

// Inline ref inside Table
Table orders {
  user_id  int  [ref: > users.id]
}

// Enums
Enum status { active inactive pending }

// TableGroups (bounded contexts)
TableGroup billing { orders invoices payments }

// Project metadata
Project my_app { Note: 'Main schema' }
```

Schema prefix is optional: `Table users` = `Table public.users`.

---

## !include — multi-file schemas

```dbmlx
!include "auth/users.dbmlx"
!include "billing/invoices.dbmlx"

Ref: users.id < invoices.user_id
```

Paths are relative to the including file. Resolved before parsing.

---

## DiagramView — named filtered views

Each view has its own layout file. Switching views in the diagram sidebar loads that view's saved positions.

```dbmlx
DiagramView auth_context {
  Tables { users, sessions, roles }
}

DiagramView billing_overview {
  TableGroups { billing }
}

DiagramView tenant_schema {
  Schemas { tenant }
}
```

Sections (`Tables`, `TableGroups`, `Schemas`) are unioned. Multiple sections = show tables matching any. Omit sections or use `*` to show all.

---

## Migration diff annotations

Annotate columns to visualize a schema migration. Annotations are stripped before parsing — they never cause errors.

```dbmlx
Table orders {
  id        int           [pk]
  amount    decimal(10,2) [add]                             // new column — green + prefix
  total     decimal       [drop]                            // removed — red strikethrough
  customer  varchar(100)  [modify: name="customer_id" type="int"]  // rename+retype — amber two-row
  status    varchar(50)   [not null] [drop]                 // combines with standard settings
}
```

- `[add]` — column is being added. Renders green with `+` prefix.
- `[drop]` — column is being removed. Renders red with strikethrough. Kept in parse for FK checks.
- `[modify: name="x" type="y"]` — rename and/or retype. Both attributes optional. Renders before→after.

---

## Layout sidecar files

Positions, viewport, group state, and edge offsets are stored alongside the schema. One file per view.

| View | File |
|---|---|
| Default | `schema.dbmlx.layout.json` |
| Named `auth_context` | `schema.dbmlx.auth_context.layout.json` |

Format: keys alphabetically sorted, integer coords, `false`/zero values omitted for minimal Git diffs.

```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "viewport": { "x": 0, "y": 0, "zoom": 1.0 },
  "tables": { "public.users": { "x": 120, "y": 80 } },
  "groups": { "billing": { "collapsed": true, "color": "#D0E8FF" } },
  "edges": {}
}
```

Commit layout files alongside schema — teammates see the same diagram on checkout.

---

## What AI assistants should know

- Generate `.dbmlx`, not `.dbml` — the file extension matters for the extension to activate.
- `[add]`/`[drop]`/`[modify:]` are safe to use — the extension strips them before the DBML parser sees them.
- `!include` paths must be relative. Do not use absolute paths.
- `DiagramView` blocks go in the root schema file, not in included files.
- TableGroup member names are unqualified (no schema prefix).
- Layout files are machine-managed — do not edit them by hand unless correcting positions.
- To scaffold a migration: copy the current table, annotate changed columns with `[add]`/`[drop]`/`[modify:]`.
